property.caption: Image attribute
property.description: Editable image attribute. The cropped image overwrites this attribute.

property.caption: Crop shape
property.description: Geometry of the crop selection. Rectangle keeps straight edges; Circle masks the result with a round cutout (transparent corners on PNG, white corners on JPEG).

property.caption: Aspect ratio
property.description: Locks the proportions of the crop selection while the user drags. Choose Free to let the user resize without constraint, a preset, or Custom to define your own ratio below.

property.caption: Custom aspect width
property.description: Numerator of the custom ratio (e.g. 3 in a 3:2 crop). Only used when Aspect ratio is set to Custom.

property.caption: Custom aspect height
property.description: Denominator of the custom ratio (e.g. 2 in a 3:2 crop). Only used when Aspect ratio is set to Custom.

property.caption: Canvas width (px)
property.description: On-screen width of the crop area in the page. The image is fit inside this box; it does not affect the resolution of the saved crop.

property.caption: Canvas height (px)
property.description: On-screen height of the crop area in the page. The image is fit inside this box; it does not affect the resolution of the saved crop.

property.caption: Resizable handles
property.description: If on, the user can drag the corners of the selection to resize it. If off, the selection stays at its initial size and can only be moved.

property.caption: Enable zoom
property.description: If on, a zoom slider is shown below the crop area so the user can scale the image up or down. If off, the image is always shown at 1× (fit-to-canvas).

property.caption: Mouse wheel zoom
property.description: Controls whether scrolling the mouse wheel over the image changes the zoom. "On (hold Ctrl)" is recommended so normal page scrolling still works.

property.caption: Minimum zoom
property.description: Smallest zoom factor the user can reach. 1 means "fit-to-canvas" (no shrinking below the visible area). Values below 1 let the user zoom out so the image is smaller than the canvas; values above 1 force the image to start zoomed-in.

property.caption: Maximum zoom
property.description: Largest zoom factor the user can reach. For example, 4 means the image can be magnified up to 4× its fit-to-canvas size, which is useful for cropping fine detail. Must be greater than Minimum zoom.

property.caption: Show preview
property.description: If on, a small live thumbnail of the current crop selection is shown next to the canvas. Useful to confirm the framing before pressing the Crop button.

property.caption: Preview width (px)
property.description: On-screen width of the live preview thumbnail. Has no effect on the saved image.

property.caption: Preview height (px)
property.description: On-screen height of the live preview thumbnail. Has no effect on the saved image.

property.caption: Output format
property.description: File format of the saved crop. PNG preserves transparency (best for circle crops) but produces larger files; JPEG is smaller but has no transparency and applies lossy compression.

property.caption: JPEG quality (0.0 - 1.0)
property.description: Compression level for JPEG output. 1.0 keeps the most detail (largest file); 0.5 is roughly average web quality. Ignored when Output format is PNG.

property.caption: Output size
property.description: Resolution of the saved crop. "Original" preserves the pixels from the source image (sharpest, larger file). "Viewport" downscales the crop to the on-screen canvas size — useful for avatars and thumbnails.

property.caption: Crop button caption
property.description: Label shown on the button that commits the crop. Defaults to "Crop"; override for translation or custom wording (e.g. "Save avatar").

property.caption: On crop
property.description: Action executed after the cropped image is committed.
